title: braya rosea var. simplicior
intro: Braya rosea var. simplicior, also known as the Alaskan mountain avens, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Arctic tundra of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including sore throats, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, due to its health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Braya rosea var. simplicior can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which have been traditionally used to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
